behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names scripts/pr-status.js, all required functions (getAllJobs, getFailedJobs, categorizeJobs, getFlakyTests, generateReport, exec, execAsync, execJson, main), module.exports, and conclusion values 'failure', 'timed_out', 'startup_failure' which tests assert on.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes WHAT is broken (silent API errors, missing conclusion types) without specifying variable names like FAILED_CONCLUSIONS or exact line changes.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for presence of required functions and conclusion strings ('failure', 'timed_out', 'startup_failure') which any correct fix would contain; no gold-specific names like FAILED_CONCLUSIONS are asserted.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute node --check, run node subprocess code that parses source, run prettier, and inspect behavior beyond just grep.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests assert specific functions exist and the full script text contains required conclusion strings; a stub couldn't satisfy prettier check plus structural checks plus conclusion tokens.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover syntax, structure, prettier, function presence, and (truncated but) conclusion strings matching instruction requirements.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments ('Gates', 'Pass-to-pass') and have descriptive names and docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task produces markdown report and script behavior, no structured API schema required.

no_typos.reason: Paths scripts/pr-status.js and function names are consistent across instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction identifies the file pr-status.js as task domain but does not point to exact lines or name the gold constant.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code-only task, not an agentmd-edits config task.
